sentences.text: Hello!
Hello!
Hi, how are you?
I have a new computer, so I had to download Zoom just to get into this call.
Good.
Sorry, you guys are teams, aren't you?
Yes.
Yeah.
But even that, it's been like, let me access your microphone and your camera, so even if they're internal, it's been a process.
That's perfect.
Us, when we do teams, we're like, oh my gosh, I don't even know what I'm doing.
Yeah.
It's like a foreign world.
And then you're the one who's presenting, too.
And it's always on team meetings, where it's, like, me and 20 of them, and I'm on a projector in their office, and I can't figure out how to share my screen.
It's always a really good start.
Yep.
Well, how've been?
How's your summer going?
Good!
Fast.
How about you?
Mine has been good and too fast as well.
I think it's, like, unseasonably hot in Wisconsin.
Is it?
Yeah, it was, and now it's in the 60s already, so it happened quick.
That's good.
I know, Arizona's kind of… it… it's in the… it's like… teasing us, it's coming.
Better weather's coming.
Yeah.
That's, like, a good break for you guys, though.
For us, I'm like, please….
Please hang on to something a little bit longer.
No, I don't blame you.
Same summer, yeah.
Yes, Adam.
It's too dark, too cold, too long out in New England.
We got scared by some cold weather, and then it hit 80, and I was like, thank you, it's like….
I do like a pumpkin spice latte, which I've heard is back, but….
I would like it to be 75 degrees for, like, another month.
back, or it's back, the pumpkin spice?
Even today.
Today was, like, the fall drink launch.
We… I don't know, I talked about this yesterday.
August 26th?
By the way, Taylor Swift got engaged, too.
Big day.
Saw that.
Whoa!
But she was engaged, like, 3 months ago, but….
Oh, really?
Oh yeah, there's no way she announced that in, like, real time.
You think she was strategic?
It is strategic, yes.
have to be.
That's a good point.
Do you think she'll change her last name?
Do you think it'd be Taylor… Taylor… Kelsey?
That's the middle name.
Kelsey?
There you go.
I think you're gonna see a Travis Swift.
Now….
Great.
I tried to take my wife's last name, but we couldn't.
She's the last of her lineage in Russian, and she was like, but in Russia.
your last name would be spelled differently than mine, because they're masculine and feminine, so… I was all for it.
I think Sam Sakaiva would sound real cool.
Oh, that'd be really cool.
It'd be intimidating.
I got this call with Sam Sakai, this guy….
I know, I think it's real letdown when the Zoom screen comes out.
Oh, God.
Speaking of which….
I might go off-camera here, because I'm just putting food… eating, so….
All good.
I have a… it gives me hope, Sam.
I hope there's more guys, like, out… out there in the world like you, because I… I have 3 daughters, so… When they get married, I would love for them to keep the last name, but….
There you go.
We'll see.
Probably.
You never know.
I'm one of them.
for now.
Never know.
How's your little one, Katrina?
Good, not so little.
You're only crazy, but you've seen my husband, so it was expected to be giant.
Is he 2 or 3?
I can't remember.
He's 3.
Okay, it's 3.
Yeah, just turned 3.
Oh.
Let's turn 3?
Not too… yeah, like, June, so….
You're entering the funnest year of your life.
Yeah.
You heard… have you been warned about 3-year-olds?
Yeah, he was an easy 2-year-old, which I've always heard, like, yeah, three major over terrible twos is worse, and….
Just living up to it.
Mine was easy when she was 2, and I was like, there's no way.
And I think the last year of my life has been the hardest year of my entire existence with her.
She's just turned 4, and she's still, like, really scary, so….
I was just gonna say, my 3-year-old just turned 4.
Sorry, Paige.
She's been way better.
Like, I think it was, like, she's….
I need a coat for you?
Maybe it's just summertime, and life is better in the summer.
But 3 was a disaster for her, but she's been better at 4.
But who knows?
Katrina, you might have the perfect 3-year-old.
He… I mean, he's got his moments, but honestly, we've been… I can't complain.
We've had it so easy and lucky with him, so… but he definitely is, like, only child at the same time, too.
Well, I know we'll get to work in a sec, but we always have to ask, how's Josh doing?
Are we fighting fires?
What are… running 6-minute miles?
What's going on?

He's doing good, he's… if you can believe it, actually, September 5th is his last day of probation, so he's almost off probation, so gearing up for the evaluations to test out, and then, heading to medic school, because he goes to be a medic, firefighter, so….
Nice.
Yeah, that's awesome.
Yeah.
It's been fun.
That's cool.

He's had a few, what, like, Jaws of life, and yeah, some different fires, and… because he's on his ladder rotation now, so that's coming with, like, a whole different set of things, and… yeah, some definitely some fun stuff.

That's on… saying we have, monsoons and haboobs out here, and that's introduced a whole new thing where he's, like, learning that… sit outside power lines until SRP can get there, because there's active lines, and trying to….
Ow.
Yeah, so it's been… So many different things.
Learned a lot.
Has he enjoyed it?
Loves it.
Yeah, so it's been a really good move.
Yeah.
Yeah.
The crew loves going to the station.
It's been fun.

Every time we go, it's like, the first time we cared about, like, the headphones in the truck, and we took him in the truck, and he didn't even realize he was in it, and the sirens are on, and then the next time we… now he has to drive it every time, or open the doors, or… it's… yeah, so it's been kind of fun to see, like, the developmental milestones, and….
That's cool.
All of that, yeah.
Very different than digital marketing.
Very, yes.
And you yourself have been promoted since we last spoke?
Yeah!
See that?
Yes, he did.
Congrats.
Thank you.
That's exciting.
Yeah.
How's that been going?
Sam's not listening right now, he sees on mute.
Right.
Give us a chance to eat, actually, yeah.
No, it's been… I'm just shoveling cottage cheese into my face, so don't mind me.
I'm listening.
Yum.
God, it's cheese!
That's great.
Brown joke, yeah.
Do we have anything on top of the cottage cheese, or is it just plain?
You know, some Wheat Thins?
I actually… I really like cottage cheese just straight up.
Might be a serial killer trait, but… I do too.
I do too.
I like it as.
I eat it with, like, a flavored chip or cracker, and then it's like….
That's good, I like that.
Or grapes with cottage cheese, like that sweet and salty.
I put a little drizzle of honey on top, and some peaches, maybe some….
We've got Hot Honey?
I have done a hot honey, I have done a hot honey, but… I'll toss a banana on top there with some honey.
It's great.
I've gotten into the fruit and cottage cheese.
Me neither.
I should try.
Katrina, we were talking about your promotion.
Now I'm… now I'm hungry.
I was like, had to talk about cottage cheese.
I was called out, Patrick, so I'm standing strong.
Anyway, congrats, Katrina.
Thank you.
Appreciate it.
Last thing before we jump in, is Kyle back in the office yet?
He's not….
Good.
He's got another….
Glad to hear that.
Good for him.
week, if not a little bit more.
I know.
He never takes time off, so that's good that he's kind of forced to take time off.
Yeah, yeah, so excited for him.
He sent me a picture of, Rory holding her.
It's so good.
Yeah, it's adorable, I know.
Yeah.
Girl.
that….
Yep.
Well, I guess we can jump into it.
So, we're thinking for today, I know we just chatted a couple months ago about this, so see where you guys are at, see how everything's going with Sochi.

We do have one update on our end that we wanted to share with you guys, but no, obviously you guys are in, like, full swing of budget season right now, so, wanted to see how things are progressing on your end.
So, before we jump in, I can share the update that we have on our end.
And one thing that we wanted to share with you guys is an update to our posts.
So, the last time we chatted, it was, 3 times a week posts, and it was mostly property-specific posts, but we did talk about starting to do a variation of post types with this product.
And we're there now, so… Posting types, still having the property-specific posts.
Wooh.
That's, like, really the basis of this product, is to get more content about the actual property out there.
But then we've had other customers asking us to layer on additional post types.
So, we have this live now, where we're posting holiday post types, lifestyle post types, tour scheduling posts, pet policy posts.
And then we are starting the local attraction posts as well.
So, we have this live with a handful of our customers, but wanted to share this to show the progression of post types that we have now.
Love this.
This is awesome.
Yeah.
Obviously, I say it's all tied to, like, Google Business, profiles, right?
Is that how it's pulling, like, some of the lifestyle and local?
I think from what we talked in the past, it's pulling from, like, proximity, Google, business profiles.
The local post, yeah, yep, yeah.
Yeah, I think the idea, Katrina, is… is we put some guardrails around, like, types of businesses.
We're starting with restaurants, and then also some guardrails on, like, reviews.
Like, we won't want to highlight anything that's less than 4.
Love that.
2 stars, but, … And then we'll branch out with more types of local attractions, but… That, is launching here in, like, the next two weeks for our customers.
The whole… design behind this product.
Iteration 1 that you saw was just property-specific posting, because we have that integration with your feed, we have your pictures.
I mean, create the posts.
Yep.

A lot of our customers love that, but also, like, hey, when you want more posting variety, and that's where we branched out to automated holidays, pet policy, scheduled tour, and local attractions as well.
So, hopefully.
Filling in more of what most of our customers expect from a social approach, while still maintaining everything to be property-specific in all of its content.
So….
How does this work now?
Because I remember it was, like, you would rotate 1, 2, 3, kind of, like, community in unit floor plan.
Do you almost, like, can you custom set, like, one pet a month, one… This a week, or do you have, like, more of a formula for this now?
What does that look like?
Yeah, so these are all the 8 posts, and these will be what you're getting.
So you get a community amenity.
Hmm, okay.
Holiday Innit floor plan, and then it kind of rotates based on the week.
Holidays don't change that often, you know, because we'll do.
Like.
Labor Day holiday, and then fill in the rest from there.
But you would get 8 posts.
I know the last time we talked, we had talked… it was 3 posts a week, and that's.
We can adjust.
If you say, hey, we need to do 3 posts, that's 12 a month, we could add some more posts in here.
Okay.
But, for the most part, it's these… these are the 8 posts you get a month, and then we rotate, kind of around the holiday post.
From there.
Because you, you were scheduling and still are scheduling everything out through Sochi, is that correct?
I know last time… We piloted this, it was… Post, maybe a little bit redundant, was the feedback.
Yeah.

Yeah, I think… was it pulling… I'm curious how it was pulling where we talked… maybe it was, like, for instance, floor plan, it kept pulling the same ones instead of rotating through, and I wonder if it was that… that was based on, like, availability.
Or, like, for instance.
photos, like, I remember this playground kept coming up, like, 3 times a week.
But maybe it was, like, because we happened to have 10 shots of the playground, but only, like.
two of other ones, like, so I'm kind of curious, like.
If maybe it's gotten a little bit more of a…
Rotate regardless versus based on availability or, like, availability of units or availability of… Resources, titled a certain way.
Yeah, maybe Paige could speak to… not to put you on the spot, Paige, we've completely overhauled our photo tagging system, the algorithm to which… to how we choose which picture is posted and when.
So, I don't know, Paige, if there's anything you want to expand on that?

Yeah, just… we've, like Patrick said, we just completely updated the process there, so we can tag photos more accurately, and then there is a system in place to make sure we're not reusing the same amenities as frequently as well, so….
Okay, cool.
Yeah, a lot smoother.
Yep, I think that was one of the pieces of feedback that Jordan, at the time, had had, that and we were… we were automating out, like, 7 days, so he was.
having.
in and check every… at least every 7 days.
I think he was even doing it more often than that, like, twice a week.
Yeah.

So now, the tool is out about 30 days, so you'll be able to go and see the next 30 days of upcoming posts, so you really only have to check it, because it's a rolling 30 days, so you really only have to check it once during that period where he was doing it at least once a week.
And what's that look like now, Katrina?
Jordan's not there anymore.
give us insight into how… has the process changed since we last spoke?
What does it look like currently for your social posting?
Hasn't changed too much.
So now we have, Emily… it used to be Emily and Jordan.
Jordan's no longer with us, so he was replaced by Liv.
And then, Emily is now corporate only, and she was replaced with Ruby, so Emily's still with us, but just doesn't touch property.
So yeah, I have two new team members, Ruby and Liv.
So, I feel like part of this is getting them onboarded.
I feel like they're onboarding enough… onboarded enough where they could participate in a pilot for us to better understand, but otherwise, not much has changed here.
I mean.
Sam and I need to review it at large, but we still focus on either 8 or 10 posts a month.

I know we've talked about the brand pull-through as far as just, like, tying in some brand elements, and that's as simple as, like, logo overlay, or color border, like, the community's colors, or it's a border or something, which I know we could, like, feed the system.
I think the hard part is knowing now our struggle, and I'm not sure what's on your roadmap here, is just the video being king.
So that's something.
I need to, like, re-evaluate, but for now, at least with our packages, it's either 1 or 2 videos a month as a part of that 8 or 10 posts.
And would these be videos that are already created, or were you hoping to create the videos within the platform?
Already created, so we're creating them, and then posting them through Sochi.

So that's a similar thing here where we could theoretically create them and post them through, because, candidly, we are… we lack video resources, whether that's, like, raw footage, from an on-site team, like, from their phone, or, just, like, video we can slice, so what we are doing is, like, creating a Canva, essentially motion graphic, but it downloads as an MP4, so that still makes it show in real, so it's really just us taking … Property photos, some stock photography, adding motion to it, whatever type of storytelling lens we want to, branding it so that it, like, comes through as a video.
Well, you answered one of the questions I was gonna have, which was the formatting, and if it's MP4, that's great.
So we're launching video, like, right now.
Nice.
So, that was also feedback.
Not the ability to make videos, but if you have the videos, you could post Direction.
Here's one for you.
One of… a big limitation for Sochi for us.
that I'm curious if you could solve for, if you haven't already, is being able to add music.
So, like, when you go into Instagram natively, you can add music to it.

That is a social limitation, so that is something, like, that's a current hurdle for us, where, you know, if I add music in Canva before I download it, and then the file itself has it, and I upload it through social, it's fine.

It'd probably work same here, but it's just an extra step, especially for our on-site teams when we're trying to push adoption and use of the platform, that, hey, you have to edit the photo on your own phone using this app or in Canva.
Versus, like, they can't go straight from phone upload, because, like, so she has an app, like, oh, I just took a video upload, add music, as kind of a current hurdle that we're dealing with.
Okay.
Yeah, I don't think we've been asked for music.
This is a first, which makes complete sense to me, so I'll have to ask.
I know the first step for us is the MP4 capabilities.
Video appears to be… If you look at, like, what large language models search like, they love video, so it appears.
Yeah.
major strategy moving forward.
And then for those Excuse me, those… properties that get video, would they be the properties that opt into your services?
Or how does that work?

Yeah, when they opt in, so we have 3 packages, so the top 2 have video, so we're creating that, but… net nets, the expectation of all of RPM that they use Sochi to post, so even if they're not in our services and they're doing it themselves, yeah, like, a lot of on-site teams are, you know, actually using their phone and creating a little walkthrough virtual tour or something, … And yeah, like I said, there's that Sochi app that they can use, too, that some people do like to leverage, just for, like, ease of….
God.
Taking footage and straight to posting, or scheduling it.
And not to get ahead of ourselves here, because I think you mentioned Pilot on the front end, which we absolutely would love to do, and, like, get you into this platform.
all goes well with the pilot.
Do you foresee a tool like this taking over the entirety of what Sochi has?
Meaning.

whether they opt into your services or not, somebody would have access to post, or could you… would you more so foresee it as taking over the opted-in service component, and that would be the aspect that you go?
Or is it TBD too soon to… to figure that out?
And I'll let Sam weigh in there.
I think it depends.
For me personally, I would love to find out if it… gives my team efficiency, so we can handle more properties, so yeah, if it makes opt-in services more efficient, that would be good.
I love the idea of it replacing Sochi, potentially, just depends on what makes sense for the company, because right now.
they're required to pay for Sochi, but if they don't use it, they get nothing.
So at least here, if they're required to, you know, opt into your platform, they're at least having it auto-generated.
Like, right now, they have to schedule posts.
So you have properties that are paying for it, doing nothing, where at least here it's a set and forget it.

Even if it's… not perfect, or we have expectations of them adding more, at least they're… we know at a bare minimum, are getting 8 posts a week that we know are of decent quality, because it's linked to the assets available for their website.
But I'll let Sam better speak to that.
That's kind of where him and I need to….
Yeah, maybe….
moment to, like, re-evaluate.
No, it's a great question.

I think it just comes down to… … We would like to consolidate tech stacks, so our teams aren't logging into multiple things, and… I think this does actually offer areas of efficiency there, but it does come down to, like, pricing and what things are bundled with which providers.
There are things that Sochi offers that you don't offer that we do utilize, and quite frankly, that's okay.
I think, like, a lot of people are offering a lot of things.
good at.
in, like, a Walmart fashion, like, I enjoy people that, like, kind of go deep in the tech areas that they do utilize, so… Yeah.

would say, like, in full transparency, like, this is really interesting, and this is compelling, so I think it's something we want to….

always on.

post.

There, too, but… … I think the… The direction of this product is definitely to help our customers realize more efficiencies, while Creating property-specific content that… is what people expect from social, but also plays into what we've seen from Google and some of these LLMs prioritizing and even Instagram signaling that having business-specific content can help you be found when people are searching for apartments, so we also try to keep that in mind for our.

Create this content.
Yes.

Well, yeah, and that's right, I mean, kind of to your point, you're… You're hitting that, but with, like, the changes in social and now it being, like, showing in search results and stuff, too, like, it has more.
impact and weight now than they feel like it did before.

… I think we should probably just show our pricing, and then maybe talk about what a pilot could look like, and what, like, a free access to this product could look like for your team, so you can kind of poke around and figure out if this is something that helps.

One?
Yes, one question for you, because I'm going… I found my old notes.
So, a few other things that I'm curious if you have a status update on is reporting.
At the time, last time we talked, you still didn't have any reporting, but that is something that is a part of Sochi, like, we have it scheduled, and it sends monthly to our operational leaders on.

Their posts, like, how many posts, and then the performance of the posts, so… that is something that would… but I know we talked about There is, though, if that's not an option, though, at least some sort of integration with our NinjaCat software, because, yeah, we have to be able to report on are posting.
And those are the two metrics, Katrina, because right now, we've gotten that request from a couple customers, our product is scoping that out.
What's active.
liked?
Is those… are those the two metrics?
Number of posts, performance of posts?
Yeah, I mean, would you add anything else, Sam?

I think I care more about, like… and I know we've talked about it, Sam, in the context for, like, ELT of, like, more so, like, even reach over impressions, but yeah, I care about, … Number of posts, because that's, like, it's a… requirement of our company, quote-unquote.

Like, they should be scheduling posts 3 times a week, which we could… Sam and I talked about, too, changing the narrative, whether that should be 2 or 3, less about that, but it's, like, that quality over quantity kind of play.
Yup.

Yep, correct.
So, I don't know if your other partners have talked about this, but I don't…

So, I would just say, like, from that standpoint.
I'm less concerned.

Okay.
Yeah, so….

our Google Business Reporting is an aggregation of your Google Analytics that you already have access to, so I'd imagine it's the same structure if you have reporting for social that you could pull from there.

Yeah.

blend in all of our reporting.

digital marketing channels, so, like, let's… Happy to let them be good at that, use.

app mention.

it could recommend a coffee shop, like, what's your go-to latte order or something, but that's okay if it doesn't have it.

polls and questions that you have, you know, like in post, I think are really interesting, and I don't know if Meta opens up to scheduling platforms, those capabilities.
